ROLAND H. CRABTREE. C.P.A. (RETIRED) 
FRED W. KARLEN. C.PA (RETIRED) 
GEORGE A. ARZoo. C.PA 
SHELLEY Z. FOX. C.PA 
KAREN A. HOLLENBECK. C.PA 
DEBRA L BURR. C.P.A. 
JERRY D. WOOD. C.P.A. 
GREGG M. KOBASHIGAWA. C.P.A. 
Kevin Braaten-Moen 
CRABTREE, KARLEN & ARZOO 
&"I:ifod :PuGua dlaeountanu 
POST OFFICE BOX 2945 - 94516 - 3145 
6355 RIVERSIDE AVENUE - 92506 - 3145 
RIVERSIDE. CALIFORNIA 
September 22, 1989 
Fair Political Practices 
428 J Street 
Commission 
Sacramento, CA 92814 
Dear Kevin: 
TELEPHONE 683-6730 
FACSIMILE 
(714) 369-8071 
I spoke with you via telep10ne on September 18 and 20, 1989, concerning 
campaign reporting based on U.S. District Judge Lawrence Karlton's recent 
decision on Proposition 73. 
The following is our' understanding of current 
requirements: 
1. Restricted funds (funds received prior to 1-1-89) can be combined with 
non-restricted funds by transferring the restricted funds into the non-
restricted fUl1ds account. 
There is no time limit on this transfer, 
however, the restricted funds must be transferred to an unrestricted 
account prior to being used for campaign purposes. 
2. 
We were in the process of filing amended campaign reports that were 
filed on form 490 and should have been on 4901\. It is our undertanding 
this has not changed. 
3. Bonds or CD's purchased from restricted accoll1ts do not have to 
immediately be cashed in and the funds transferred to an Ul1restricted 
accoUl1t, but at which time the bonds are cashed or CD's mature, the 
funds must be deposited into an Ul1restricted account before further 
disposition. 
4. 
On the next campaign filing report the balance of any funds remalnL'1g 
in restricted accounts should be shown as transferred on the reports 
even though there may still be funds in those accounts. Specifically, 
if a CD purchased with restricted funds has not matured by the mext 
campaign report, the restricted funds report should still ShOVl a 
transfer to an Ul1restricted account to zero out the restricted campaign 
fund report. 
If any of the preceeding are not correct 
to a 
(Le. 
#4), please contact our office at (714) 683-6730. 
Your assistance has been 
most 
Sincerely, 
cc: 
Tom Mullin 
Friends of Senator Robert Presley 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Jerry D. Wood, CPA 
Crabtree, Karlen & Arzoo 
P.O. Box 2945 
September 27, 1989 
Riverside, DA 
94516-3145 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-554 
Dear Mr. Wood: 
We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice 
under the political Reform Act on September 25, 1989. 
Your letter 
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact 
that division directly at (916) 322-5662. 
If the letter is ~ppropriate for confirmation without fUrther 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response wili be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, review 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660

sections.question_synthetic: The requester seeks FPPC confirmation of advice regarding campaign reporting requirements under Proposition 73, specifically concerning the treatment of restricted funds, amended campaign reports, investments purchased with restricted funds, and reporting of fund transfers.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both the original incoming request letter (dated September 22, 1989) and the FPPC's acknowledgment/receipt letter (dated September 27, 1989). The incoming request details four specific understandings about campaign fund requirements that the requester wants confirmed. This is not yet the FPPC's substantive response letter, only the intake acknowledgment.

embedding.summary: This is an incoming request letter from CPA Jerry D. Wood at Crabtree, Karlen & Arzoo to the FPPC seeking confirmation of advice on campaign reporting requirements related to Proposition 73, with four specific points about restricted fund transfers, amended report forms, bonds/CDs, and reporting balances. The FPPC's September 27, 1989 acknowledgment letter indicates the request was assigned to the Technical Assistance and Analysis Division for response.
